candidate_structures.economic_hypothesis: Aggressive traders who force a new past-only 60-minute extreme but cannot hold a five-minute close beyond it are trapped against replenishing passive liquidity and pay the first reversal leg.
candidate_structures.who_pays: Late aggressive breakout entrants whose flow fails to secure price acceptance.
candidate_structures.signal: At a completed five-minute boundary, price traded beyond the prior completed 60-minute high or low, five-minute absolute signed aggressor fraction is at or above its past-only 20-block q75, and the close returned inside the prior extreme.
candidate_structures.entry: next one-minute open after feature availability

candidate_structures.economic_hypothesis: An extreme five-minute aggressor imbalance isolated in the micro or mini contract without confirmation by its fungible sibling reflects participant segmentation rather than broad information and mean-reverts.
candidate_structures.who_pays: Contract-specific urgent traders whose imbalance is not confirmed in the fungible sibling market.
candidate_structures.signal: At a completed synchronized five-minute boundary, one contract's absolute signed fraction is at or above its past-only 20-block q90 while the sibling signed fraction has the opposite sign or absolute magnitude below its past-only median.
candidate_structures.entry: next one-minute open in the candidate product

candidate_structures.economic_hypothesis: After three completed same-side five-minute flow blocks, an extreme opposite flow block marks an inventory-control transition whose urgent execution persists for the next short horizon.
candidate_structures.who_pays: Participants anchored to the exhausted prior flow regime and liquidity providers repricing the inventory transition.
candidate_structures.signal: The prior three completed five-minute signed-flow blocks share a sign; the current completed block flips sign and its absolute signed fraction exceeds the past-only 20-block q75.
candidate_structures.entry: next one-minute open after feature availability

candidate_structures.economic_hypothesis: Two completed five-minute closes beyond the prior completed 30-minute volume-weighted value with confirming aggressor flow reveal benchmark and hedge demand that persists as session value migrates.
candidate_structures.who_pays: Inventory holders fading a value migration before benchmark and hedge demand completes.
candidate_structures.signal: Two consecutive completed five-minute closes lie on the same side of the prior completed 30-minute rolling VWAP and both five-minute signed-flow aggregates confirm that side.
candidate_structures.entry: next one-minute open after the second confirming block

CONTRE: The two date-matched August blocks cannot establish year-round microstructure stability, and mini/micro divergence can be a cost or sizing artifact despite explicit normalization.
